title: Ireland: Glendalough, Kilkenny, Cashel, Dingle & the Blaskets

main_route.excerpt: At 10.10. we set off, all 7 of us, for Ireland.
We had sandwiches for lunch near Nescliffe in Shropshire.
On to Anglesey, where we had tea next to Telford's Bridge.

main_route.original_wording: an Anglesey campsite, looking across to Holy Island
main_route.excerpt: On to an Anglesey campsite, looking across to Holy Island.

main_route.inference_reason: Anglesey campsite the night before the 9am ferry to Dún Laoghaire; Holyhead is the Anglesey ferry port for Dún Laoghaire
main_route.excerpt: The ferry to Dun Laoghaire left at 9. We rushed through breakfast & packing up & got there in good time.
The ferry to Dun Laoghaire left at 9.
We have driven to Roundwood the highest village in Ireland
we drove in a soggy state + saw the Japanese Garden + Irish National Stud at Kildare.

main_route.original_wording: Nore Valley Bonnet Bridge, Nr Kilkenny
main_route.excerpt: until we reached Nore Valley Bonnet Bridge, Nr Kilkenny at 7pm.
We left for Kilkenny, a mediaeval town. The Castle had a design centre
On to Rock of Cashel. The Kings of Munster lived on the Rock of Cashel for 600 years

main_route.normalized_name: Vale of Aherlow, County Tipperary, Ireland
main_route.excerpt: 7.15. pm we arrived at Vale of Aherlow. The campsite house + restaurant is in the stables
We shopped in Mitchel town.
We drove on to the Gallarus campsite, but it was very full ... we found another site nearer Dingle. It has it's own bay for swimming.
It was windy but not raining as we left for Dingle & saw the marvellous aquarium.
We went to the Dunquin [Dun Chaoin] Blasket Island Centre.
We drove across the peninsula through the hills to Camp. Many static vans.
We spent a good 2 hours at the Tralee Aquadome ... then on to "Geraldine's Tralee", a train car ride through a reconstructed street
Off across the Shannon ferry. Elegant Victorian pier passed.
Straight roads to Lahinch campsite in Clare.
On to Clonmacnoise. (Gathering of the Sons of Noye.) ... Frequently sacked by the Vikings
We have come on to First Lough Ree campsite near Athlone, on a very large lake formed by the Shannon.
We left at 10.00. & stopped at Grandard for elevenses ... climbed up Grandard Motte
We arrived at Killyleagh at 5.15., had a cup of tea & a swim.
En route we had a tour of Knowth where the Solstice was celebrated before even the pyramids.
It was a race down to Dun Laoghaire. Appalling signs through Dublin.

main_route.inference_reason: return ferry from Dún Laoghaire lands at Holyhead; diary notes driving through Anglesey on the way home
main_route.excerpt: We drove through Anglesey
As Simon was driving on the motorway near Meole Brace, south of Shrewsbury, the engine died after a strong smell of petrol.
We got home at 11, the van on a truck & the rest of us in the cab.

crossings.excerpt: The ferry to Dun Laoghaire left at 9.
crossings.note: Dún Laoghaire named explicitly; Holyhead departure inferred from the Anglesey campsite the night before.

crossings.excerpt: Off across the Shannon ferry. Elegant Victorian pier passed.
crossings.note: Internal Irish car ferry across the Shannon estuary (Killimer–Tarbert), between Tralee/Kerry and Clare. Not in ferries.json; recorded verbatim.

crossings.excerpt: It was a race down to Dun Laoghaire. Appalling signs through Dublin.
crossings.note: Return Irish Sea crossing; Dún Laoghaire named, lands at Holyhead (diary mentions driving through Anglesey afterwards).

stops.original_wording: Glendalough monastery (Glen of the 2 lakes); Gleann Da Loch, Co Chill Mhantáin
stops.excerpt: hope to go to Glendalough monastery tomorrow. (Glen of the 2 lakes)

excursions.label: Ventry Bay, Dún Chaoin Blasket Centre & boat to the Great Blasket

stops.excerpt: We overshot the camp site on the way home & the boys played in Ventry Bay.

stops.normalized_name: Dunquin Pier (Dún Chaoin), County Kerry, Ireland
stops.excerpt: We had lunch in the carpark & drove to Dunquin Quay. We walked down the steep ramp

stops.normalized_name: Great Blasket Island, County Kerry, Ireland
stops.excerpt: A small boat took us into the little Blasket harbour ... 12 people live on the island in the summer.

excursions.label: Tralee day en route (Aquadome, Geraldine's Tralee) before crossing to Clare
Cliffs of Moher, Doolin & the Burren / Aillwee Cave day

stops.normalized_name: Cliffs of Moher, County Clare, Ireland
stops.excerpt: set off for Moher the Cliffs of Moher. Lovely countryside ... Sheer cliffs to the sea + tower
We went to the music shop at Dolin where John bought a pipe.
At Aillwee Mountain we went into the Cave. Remains of 2 bear pits
Tea on the Burren – amazing limestone plateaus with clefts
We stopped by the road next to Leamaneh Castle, + reached the pub, The Archway, at 8.45.
It was in Ennistymon, 3 miles from campsite.

excursions.label: Ennis Friary on the way to Clonmacnoise (Lough Ree base)

stops.excerpt: Simon went to shop in Ennis ... there was just time to see the friary, built by the O'Briens, for Franciscans.

excursions.label: Christening weekend around Killyleagh: Portaferry & Seaforde House

stops.excerpt: We had a meal & saw rock pools at Portaferry.
On the Saturday we had a picnic, rocket launching & rounders & swimming at Seaforde House

boundary_flags.issue: Mixed-start spread: page A_0179_R opens with the tail of the previous trip (a German birthday party, leaving for home Thursday July 17th) before this Ireland trip begins under the heading 'Saturday August 3rd'. The Ireland trip starts at that heading.
boundary_flags.excerpt: At 4.45. after a German birthday party we left for home on Thursday July 17th ... Saturday August 3rd At 10.10. we set off, all 7 of us, for Ireland.

summary: August 1996 family camping tour of Ireland: ferry from Holyhead to Dún Laoghaire, then a clockwise loop via Glendalough, Kildare, Kilkenny, the Rock of Cashel and the Vale of Aherlow to the Dingle Peninsula, with a boat trip to the Great Blasket. They crossed the Shannon car ferry to Clare for the Cliffs of Moher, the Burren and Aillwee Cave, then turned inland via Clonmacnoise and Lough Ree before finishing at a family christening in Killyleagh, Co Down, returning home via Knowth and the Dún Laoghaire–Holyhead ferry.
extraction_notes: Stop order follows the hand-drawn numbered route map on A_0186_R (sketch outranks narrative). The diary dates are internally inconsistent (some entries carry stray ISO values like 1991 or 1997, and a 'Sat 16th August' heading appears mid-trip on A_0184_L before later correct dates), but A_0186_R titles the trip 'Ireland August 1996' and dated entries (Fri 9th Aug, Mon 12th, Tue 13th, Wed 14th, Thu 15th, Mon Aug 19) confirm 1996; date_start 1996-08-03 from 'Saturday August 3rd' and date_end 1996-08-19 from 'Monday August 19'. Overnight bases used for the main route: Anglesey campsite, Roundwood, Nore Valley/Bennettsbridge (nr Kilkenny), Vale of Aherlow, a Dingle-peninsula site near Dingle, Camp, Lahinch, Lough Ree (nr Athlone), and Killyleagh. Glendalough is treated as a day excursion from the Roundwood base (the diary plans it 'tomorrow' from Roundwood and the afternoon is spent 'back at the campsite'). 'Bonnet Bridge' is Bennettsbridge, Co Kilkenny (Nore Valley campsite). The Shannon ferry is an internal Irish crossing (Killimer–Tarbert) not present in ferries.json, recorded with route_key null. Killyleagh and the christening-weekend excursions (Portaferry, Seaforde) are in Northern Ireland (countrycodes gb). Sketches A_0180_L (Telford's Menai Bridge) and A_0182_R (Rock of Cashel) are architectural sketches that corroborate stops but do not establish route order; A_0186_R is the route map. Numbered map items 1-18 all appear as stops here either in the main route or as excursions.
